days.title: Arrival / Shibuya
days.subtitle: With Dave & Gail. You land at 3pm, clear customs ~4pm, N'EX to Shibuya ~6pm. Tonight is about shaking off jet lag with a low-key first dinner.
days.summary: You just crossed the Pacific. Tonight's the soft landing — beef cutlet where you grill it yourself at the table, then a 50-year-old underground listening bar with Suntory highballs. Maybe a nightcap in the tiny Nonbei Yokocho bar alley if you're still standing. No pressure, no agenda. Just shake off the jet lag and let Tokyo sink in.

activities.name: 3:00 PM
activities.description: Land at Narita. Clear immigration/customs

activities.name: ~4:30 PM
activities.description: Board Narita Express (N'EX) to Shibuya (direct)

activities.name: ~6:00 PM
activities.description: Arrive Shibuya Station. Walk to hotel. Check in, drop bags, freshen up

sections.title: Dinner
sections.subtitle: No reservations needed for either. Both cash-friendly.

activities.name: Gyukatsu Motomura Shibuya
activities.description: Beef cutlet — 5-10 min walk from hotel. DIY grilling at your table — fun, interactive, perfect first-night-in-Japan energy. Arrive by 7pm

activities.name: Fuunji
activities.description: Tsukemen (dipping ramen) — Near Shinjuku South Exit (~15-20 min from hotel: JR Yamanote 1 stop to Shinjuku; 5-8 min walk). Rich, intense fish-based dipping noodles. Cash only, vending machine order. If you're feeling energetic after the flight
activities.lineUpTip: Arrive by 7pm to minimize wait (typically 15-30 min on weeknights).

activities.name: Grandfather's
activities.description: Underground listening bar since 1971. Owner Toru Ishikawa has curated 2,000+ albums of 70s/80s Billboard hits. Vintage sound system, dark wood, dim lights, Suntory highball. This is the source of Japan's listening bar culture. Walking distance from hotel. Perfect low-key first night

activities.name: Nonbei Yokocho
activities.description: Tiny bar alley ~10 min walk from hotel (opposite side of Shibuya Station). Grab a nightcap at one of the pint-sized standing bars. No cover, just point and order. One drink is — do both Grandfather's AND Nonbei Yokocho

logistics.text: At Narita: Buy physical Suica card OR set up Apple Wallet Suica before landing (load 3,000-5,000 yen)
N'EX one-way ticket: buy at JR counter in arrivals
Carry cash tonight — Motomura, Fuunji, and most alley bars are cash-only
7-Eleven ATMs accept foreign cards

days.title: Chopsticks / Tsukiji / Shibuya Sky / Shabu-Shabu
days.subtitle: With Dave & Gail. Light morning, chopstick workshop first, Tsukiji snack-lunch crawl, walkable temple/shopping, sunset at Shibuya Sky, then shabu-shabu in the same building.
days.summary: A relaxed start after the arrival day. Coffee in the neighborhood, then chopstick workshop kicks off the day at 10am sharp. From Ginza, walk to Tsukiji to slow-graze the outer market stalls (10am Sunday opening) — that's lunch. Wander walkable temple/shrine (Tsukiji Hongwanji, Namiyoke Inari, Hama-rikyu) and hit knife/watch shops on the way back through Ginza. Sunset at Shibuya Sky at 6:20 (already booked), then take the elevator down to A5 wagyu shabu-shabu at Tsukada inside Shibuya Scramble Square.

morningCoffee.name: Sakuragaoka at Cerulean Tower Tokyu Hotel
morningCoffee.description: Walking distance from Granbell. Traditional Japanese breakfast buffet (grilled fish, rice, miso, tofu, tamagoyaki, pickles, fruit) plus coffee. **7:00 AM start** (LO 10:00, closes 10:30). Walk-in OK — buffet is separate from the lunch/dinner reservation system. ~¥4,200/pp. *Skipping Streamer Coffee today — buffet covers coffee.*

sections.title: Late Morning — Chopstick Workshop in Ginza

activities.name: Chopstick Making Workshop (Ginza Hashikura)
activities.description: For all 4. premium wood. Take home same day

sections.title: Midday — Tsukiji Outer Market — Snack Lunch

activities.name: Tsukiji Outer Market
activities.description: Opens ~10am Sundays. This IS lunch — graze the stalls slowly

sections.title: Early Afternoon — Walkable Shopping + Temple/Shrine
sections.subtitle: All within ~5-15 min walk of Tsukiji except the Ginza watch shops, which are ~15 min walk back toward Ginza. Houseki Hiroba is in Shibuya — easier on a separate day.

activities.name: Masamoto knives
activities.description: Knives since 1850s, at the Tsukiji market edge. Tax-free with passport. Verify Sunday hours via web search before going

activities.name: Tsukiji Hongwanji Temple
activities.description: Striking Indian-influenced Buddhist temple, ~5 min walk south of the outer market. Free

activities.name: Namiyoke Inari Shrine
activities.description: "Wave-warding" Shinto shrine right at the outer market entrance. Free

activities.name: Hama-rikyu Gardens
activities.description: Tidal pond garden, 10 min walk south of Tsukiji

activities.name: Grand Seiko Flagship Boutique Ginza Namiki
activities.description: New Grand Seiko, 15 min walk back toward Ginza. Tax-free with passport

activities.name: Watch CTI Ginza
activities.description: Vintage Grand Seiko and King Seiko, B1F near Ginza-itchome Station

activities.name: Shellman Ginza
activities.description: Museum-quality vintage. Patek, Breguet, Grand Seiko

activities.name: Shibuya Sky
activities.description: Arrive 6:00 to check in. Golden hour into city lights from 229m above Shibuya Crossing. Sunset ~6:23 PM

activities.name: Shibuya Crossing
activities.description: Experience it at street level on the way down

activities.name: Shabu-Shabu Tsukada
activities.description: 12F Shibuya Scramble Square — same building as Shibuya Sky, just take the elevator down. A5 wagyu individual hot pots. View of Scramble Crossing from the seats. Walk-in or call ahead for a reservation when you wake up

activities.name: USHIHACHI Kiwami Shibuya
activities.description: Wagyu yakiniku, Scramble Square restaurant floor (same building)

activities.name: Katsukura Shinjuku Takashimaya
activities.description: Kyoto-style tonkatsu, 14F Takashimaya Times Square. 1 stop on JR Yamanote. Walk-in. Open until 10 PM (LO 9 PM)

activities.name: Gyukatsu Motomura Shibuya
activities.description: Beef cutlet, DIY stone-grilling. ~10 min walk from Scramble Square

activities.name: JBS Vinyl Bar
activities.description: No cover, open 2pm-11pm. Wall of vinyl, great sound system. Walking distance from hotel

activities.name: SG Club
activities.description: Speakeasy cocktails. Two floors: casual downstairs (Guzzle), refined upstairs (Sip). Walkable from JBS

logistics.text: Tsukiji Outer Market opens ~10am on Sundays — that's why we flipped the order: chopsticks first, then Tsukiji as snack-lunch.
Nakajima (Michelin sardine) is closed Sundays — moved to Apr 28 lunch instead.
Shabu-Shabu Tsukada is in the same building as Shibuya Sky (Shibuya Scramble Square, 12F) — no transit between sunset and dinner.
Pre-Golden Week — today and tomorrow are your best days for harder-to-book restaurants.
Shibuya Sky ✅ BOOKED 6:20 PM

days.title: Nakameguro / Daikanyama / teamLab / Aoyama
days.subtitle: With Dave & Gail. Morning donuts and coffee, Michelin sushi lunch, teamLab Planets in the afternoon, teppanyaki dinner. Quick Kurono Tokyo (watches) stop in Aoyama before dinner.
days.summary: The splurge day. Morning starts with Japan's most hyped donuts in Nakameguro, then the real event: Michelin-starred sushi omakase at a concrete gallery counter. Quick stops at Daikanyama T-Site and the Nanamica flagship (North Face Purple Label). Afternoon is teamLab Planets barefoot with Dave and Gail. Squeeze in Kurono Tokyo (Aoyama, closes 7pm) before teppanyaki at Ukaitei. Cap with Shimokitazawa vinyl if anything's left.

morningCoffee.name: Onibus Coffee Nakameguro
morningCoffee.description: Beautiful converted house, excellent light-roast pour-over. Opens 9am.

activities.name: I'm Donut? Nakameguro
activities.description: Opens 10am. Brad's donut destination. Sells out by early afternoon. Weekend lines can be 45-60 min by midday
activities.lineUpTip: Arrive by 9:30am (30 min before opening). Sells out by early afternoon. Weekend lines can be 45-60 min by midday.

activities.name: Daikanyama T-Site (Tsutaya Books)
activities.description: Award-winning bookstore complex. Beautiful architecture, great cafe. Worth. No reservation needed

activities.name: Okura Daikanyama
activities.description: Japanese indigo-dyed goods and denim

activities.name: Nanamica Daikanyama
activities.description: Flagship store with North Face Purple Label. 26-13 Sarugakucho. Opens 11:00 AM. ~3 min walk from T-Site. — leave by 11:35 to make Udatsu at 11:55

activities.name: Udatsu Sushi
activities.description: 1 Michelin star, Nakameguro — Lunch omakase. "Art x Sushi" in a concrete gallery space. Smart casual attire required — no T-shirts, shorts, or sandals. Arrive 5 min before reservation

activities.name: Sushi SHIN
activities.description: 1 Michelin star, Nishiazabu — Lunch omakase. Book via WhatsApp/email. 10+ consecutive years Michelin. Great backup if Udatsu is full

activities.name: teamLab Planets Tokyo
activities.description: WITH DAVE & GAIL. Immersive digital art. Barefoot required. Take Yurikamome to Shin-Toyosu Station
activities.notes: ADVANCE TICKETS: Book NOW at teamlab.art/e/planets/ — Golden Week dates sell out weeks ahead. Book for ~3:00 PM slot. ~3,200 yen/pp

activities.name: Kurono Tokyo Aoyama Salon
activities.description: MUST VISIT TODAY. Closed Sun; national holidays — Apr 29 (Showa Day) is closed, so Mon Apr 27 is the only viable day. Mon-Sat 11:00-19:00. Hajime Asaoka's Kurono Tokyo watches. Phone +81-3-6823-8358

activities.name: Ukaitei Omotesando
activities.description: Splurge teppanyaki for 4 — Private room with dedicated chef. This is the memorable group experience. Dress nice-ish (smart casual)

activities.name: Ebisu Yokocho
activities.description: Izakaya alley near Ebisu Station — Multiple stalls, locals-heavy, great vibe. Perfect if teppanyaki feels like too much after a big sushi lunch

activities.name: Shimokitazawa
activities.description: 3 min train from Shibuya

activities.name: Little Soul Cafe
activities.description: Vinyl bar with soul/funk/jazz records

activities.name: Basement Bar
activities.description: Live music venue, check schedule

logistics.text: #FFFFFFT (White T-Shirt Store) — Open Sun 12-6pm! 2-3-5 Sendagaya. ~10 min walk from Harajuku/Omotesando. If Brad wants to pop in, squeeze it between teamLab and dinner.
Kurono Tokyo is closed Sun/holidays — moved to a weekday
teamLab: wear shorts/capris, everything below the knee gets wet. No socks. Lockers provided.

days.title: Glitch / Nakajima / Sumo / Kappabashi / Sensoji / Pit Inn
days.subtitle: With Dave & Gail. Big day: Glitch coffee, Nakajima Michelin sardine lunch on the first wave, 12:30 sumo show, knife shopping, quick Sensoji, then Pit Inn jazz + late izakaya. Last full weekday before Golden Week.
days.summary: Coffee nerd paradise in the morning — 3-bean tasting flight at Glitch. Lunch is the cheapest Michelin meal in Tokyo (Nakajima sardine set, Tue-Sat only), eaten on the 11:30 first wave so we can race ten minutes north for a 12:30 sumo show. Knife shopping on Kappabashi and a quick Sensoji stop in Asakusa, then back to Shinjuku for Suga Dairo Trio at Pit Inn at 7:30, late izakaya at Omoide Yokocho, and vinyl jazz at Jazz Bar Samurai to close.

morningCoffee.name: Glitch Coffee Jimbocho
morningCoffee.description: THE coffee destination. Order the 2-3 bean tasting flight (~1,500-2,000 yen). Opens 7:30am weekdays.

activities.name: Glitch Coffee flight
activities.description: (opens 7:30am weekdays). Don't linger — first-wave Nakajima requires us in Shinjuku by 11:00

sections.title: Late Morning — Subway to Shinjuku, Queue Nakajima

activities.name: PRIORITY: First-wave Nakajima
activities.description: Queue by 11:00 to be seated at 11:30 opening. Eat in flat. Sardine set, cash. Closed Sun+Mon — today (Tue) is the only viable day

activities.name: Kappabashi Street
activities.description: Kitchen Town. Knife shopping district, ~5 min walk south of Tawaramachi Stn

activities.name: Tsubaya
activities.description: Widest selection, handles most knives freely. Est. 1956

activities.name: Kamata Hakensha
activities.description: Master sharpener, every knife gets a perfect edge

activities.name: Sensoji Temple
activities.description: Tokyo's oldest temple, free. Thunder Gate (Kaminarimon), Nakamise shopping street, main hall. quick visit (skip the slow Nakamise browse — keep moving)

sections.title: Evening Pre-Show — Quick Bite Near Pit Inn

activities.name: Shinjuku Pit Inn
activities.description: Open 7:00, show 7:30. (incl. one drink). Reservation gives seating at the 7:00 door open. Apr 28, 7:30 PM

activities.name: Omoide Yokocho
activities.description: Atmospheric tiny izakaya alley, smoky yakitori stalls, beer; sake. Open well past midnight. any stall with seats — the vibe is the meal

activities.name: Jazz Bar Samurai
activities.description: Shinjuku 3-chome, 5F. 2,500+ maneki-neko cats, vinyl jazz since 1979. Drinks. Unlike any bar you've ever been to

logistics.text: Udon Maruka had to drop today (incompatible with sumo timing). Closed all of Golden Week — out for the trip.
Eat Nakajima FAST (~30 min) on the first wave. Leaving by 12:00 is non-negotiable for the 12:30 sumo check-in.
Arrange takkyubin luggage forwarding at hotel front desk this evening (send tomorrow morning, arrives Kyoto Apr 30).
Tomorrow is Showa Day (Apr 29) — Nakajima, Bird Land, most family-run spots closed.

days.title: Showa Day — Meiji / Nakano / Mofusand / Samurai / Watch Hunt / Late Eats
days.subtitle: With Dave & Gail. Showa Day = Golden Week Day 1. Brad: Meiji + Nakano + Mofusand. Alyona + Gail: onsen morning. Group converges for 2pm Samurai. Afternoon: Houseki Hiroba (priority), Loft, Beams. Late cheap dinner. Pack and bed — 8:30 AM hotel departure tomorrow for the 10:12 Nozomi to Kyoto.
days.summary: Last day in Tokyo. The morning splits cleanly: Brad does Meiji Shrine and Yoyogi Park first thing for the Showa Day vibe, then trains to Nakano Broadway for vintage watches at Jackroad, Kamekichi, and Fire Kids. Alyona and Gail head to Thermae-Yu for a long morning soak. Reunion at 2pm for the Samurai Restaurant show in Kabukicho (food included). Afternoon is the priority watch hunt at Houseki Hiroba in Shibuya, then Loft + Beams. Light early-snack breakfast, lunch is the Samurai bento, dinner is late and cheap — a Shibuya yokocho or DIY okonomyaki — before rain rolls in at 7.

sections.title: Brad's Morning — Meiji Shrine → Nakano → Mofusand

activities.name: Jackroad
activities.description: Largest dealer at Nakano, multilingual, deep Rolex/Grand Seiko inventory. Opens 11:00

activities.name: Kamekichi
activities.description: 3,000+ watches, Grand Seiko specialist. Opens 11:00

activities.name: Fire Kids
activities.description: Curated Japanese vintage

activities.name: Samurai Restaurant
activities.description: Former Robot Restaurant location

activities.name: 4:00-5:30 [Houseki Hiroba Shibuya](https://maps.google.com/?q=Houseki+Hiroba+Shibuya+Tokyo)
activities.description: 5 min from hotel. Massive secondhand watch inventory: Rolex, Grand Seiko, Omega. ~5,000 watches refurbished and overhauled. Open 11:00-19:30 daily, walk-in. Tax-free with passport. If Houseki has the piece → done. If not, fallback to Grand Seiko Boutique Kyoto Shijo on Apr 30 afternoon (Nishiki Market neighborhood), or Osaka Shinsaibashi on May 5 (world's largest Seiko/Grand Seiko store)

activities.name: 5:30-6:00 [Loft Shibuya](https://maps.google.com/?q=Loft+Shibuya+21-1+Udagawacho+Tokyo)
activities.description: 21-1 Udagawacho, 3 min from Shibuya Stn. 6 floors of Japanese design goods, stationery, kitchenware. Open 10am-9pm. Focused 30-min browse

activities.name: 6:00-6:45 [Beams House Shibuya](https://maps.google.com/?q=Beams+House+Shibuya+Hikarie+8F)
activities.description: Shibuya Hikarie 8F, ~5 min walk from Loft. Beams' Shibuya outpost — fashion, lifestyle, accessories. Smaller than Beams Japan Shinjuku flagship but closer to hotel and rain-shelter friendly

sections.title: Dinner
sections.subtitle: Rain is forecast 7-9 PM. Stay close to hotel and pick covered/indoor.

activities.name: Shibuya Yokocho at Miyashita Park
activities.description: Indoor yokocho on the Miyashita Park complex, ~5 min walk from hotel. Multiple regional Japanese yatai stalls (Hokkaido, Osaka, Kyushu, etc.) — yakitori, takoyaki, oden, regional bites. Covered, perfect rain shelter. Cheap; delicious; variety

activities.name: Sakuratei Harajuku
activities.description: DIY okonomyaki (you cook on a hot plate at the table). Fun group experience. 3 min train to Harajuku; 5 min walk. Use covered routes via Shibuya Stn underground passages if rain is heavy

activities.name: Tonkasu Negiyaki Yamamoto Shibuya
activities.description: Hiroshima-style okonomyaki, ~10 min walk from hotel

activities.name: CoCo Ichibanya Shibuya
activities.description: Curry chain, multiple Shibuya locations. Curry katsu, customizable spice level

activities.name: Tendon Tenya Shibuya
activities.description: Tempura don chain. Cheap and fast. Multiple Shibuya locations

logistics.text: 8:30 AM hotel departure tomorrow (Apr 30) — Shinkansen Nozomi 23 at 10:12 AM from Tokyo Station. Pack day bag tonight. Takkyubin luggage to Kyoto should already be arranged.
Showa Day closures: Nakajima, Udon Maruka, Bird Land, most family-run spots closed. Sumo, watch dealers, Beams, Loft, Mofusand, Thermae-Yu, Samurai Restaurant all open.
Samurai Restaurant Apr 29 2:00 PM: ~¥9,000/pp. Book on Klook/GetYourGuide/Viator for 4. Show ~90 min. Includes bento/meal — this IS lunch.
Brad's Nakano Broadway morning: Watch dealers open 11:00 AM. Phone/IG-check Jackroad or Kamekichi if a specific piece matters. Cash gives leverage; Ginza shops are more fixed pricing.

Thermae-Yu Shinjuku for Alyona + Gail: 11:00 AM-9:00 AM next day operation; tattoo cover seals ¥300 each, max 2 per person. Large tattoos may be denied — call 03-5285-1726 day-of to confirm. Holiday rate ~¥2,800/pp.
Rain forecast 7-9 PM: Plan dinner indoor / covered. Shibuya Yokocho at Miyashita Park is the easy rain-friendly pick.
~~Henry's Burger Harami reservation: DM @henrysburger_japan~~ — Not booked. Lunch dropped from Apr 29; Samurai Restaurant 2pm includes the meal.
~~Nishikawa Pillow Fitting~~ — CANCELLED (not practical for 2-week trip).
Flower Mountain shoes — Save for Osaka's Tenjinbashi store (on the same shopping street as Harukoma sushi).
Beams Japan Shinjuku flagship — Bigger Beams option in Shinjuku 3-chome, but Beams House Shibuya works on the way home tonight without a Shinjuku detour.

days.title: Wednesday, April 30 — Tokyo to Kyoto — Travel Day / Nishiki Market / Gion
days.subtitle: Dave & Gail depart (or stay in Tokyo). Brad & Alyona take Nozomi 23 to Kyoto. Arrive 12:23 PM.
days.summary: Travel day — say goodbye to Dave and Gail, grab an ekiben bento, and bullet-train to Kyoto. You arrive just after noon and the vibe shifts completely. Afternoon is Nishiki Market grazing, Japanese jazz vinyl shopping on Teramachi Street, and maybe your first look at SOU SOU haori jackets. Evening is 560-year-old soba, a walk through Gion at dusk, and Pontocho alley along the river.

morningCoffee.name: Quick coffee near Shibuya Station or Tokyo Station before the train. Streamer Coffee Shibuya opens 8am weekdays.

activities.name: 9:15 AM
activities.description: Leave Shibuya Granbell. Take JR Yamanote Line to Tokyo Station

activities.name: 9:45 AM
activities.description: Arrive Tokyo Station. Find Tokaido Shinkansen platforms (14-19). Buy ekiben (train bento) for the ride

activities.name: 10:12 AM
activities.description: Board Nozomi 23

activities.name: 12:23 PM
activities.description: Arrive Kyoto Station

activities.name: Nishiki Market
activities.description: 5 min walk north from hotel. "Kyoto's Kitchen." 120+ shops

activities.name: Aritsugu Knives
activities.description: Inside Nishiki Market. Est. 1560, originally swordsmiths

activities.name: Kuoe Watches
activities.description: Teramachi Shopping District. Custom vintage-inspired watches from. (Closed Tue — Wed is fine)

activities.name: Japanese Jazz Vinyl — Teramachi Record Shop Crawl
Hard Bop
activities.description: THE jazz vinyl destination. 30+ year jazz-only shop. Japanese Blue Note pressings, original US pressings, mint reissues. Old-school owner, cash preferred. Opens 12:30pm

activities.name: 100000t alonetoco
activities.description: Eclectic digger's paradise: jazz, city pop, psych rock, funk. Same building as Workshop Records. Opens ~12pm

activities.name: Workshop Records
activities.description: Jazz; rock, fair prices. Same building as 100000t

activities.name: SOU SOU Kyoto
activities.description: Modern Japanese fashion brand, 1 min from Kawaramachi Station on Shinkyogoku-dori. Nine shops clustered together — the men's line (SOU SOU Kei-i) carries modern haori jackets with bold original textile prints. Not costume-y at all — designed to wear with jeans. Also iconic split-toe tabi shoes and textiles. Hours: 11am-8pm. Right on the way between Nishiki and Teramachi — pop in while record shopping. For a full haori shopping tour hitting multiple stores, save that for Day 9

sections.title: Lunch — Keep Open / Flexible
sections.subtitle: Just Brad & Alyona now. Be flexible with Nishiki grazing as your "lunch" or sit down somewhere:

activities.name: Honke Owariya
activities.description: 560-year-old soba institution — Founded 1465. Horai Soba: 5-tier soba serving. Walk-in

activities.name: Wagyu Ramen Julia
activities.description: Wagyu beef broth ramen — Hidden gem in Gion. Quick and hearty

activities.name: Nishiki Market grazing
activities.description: Skip sit-down entirely, eat your way through for

activities.name: Honke Owariya
activities.description: If you didn't do lunch here. The 5-tier Horai Soba is ceremonial and beautiful. The building alone is worth the visit

activities.name: Omen Pontocho
activities.description: Kyoto's beloved udon — Walking distance. Closed 2nd/3rd Thursdays. (Apr 30 is a Thu — check if 5th Thursday, should be fine)

activities.name: Gion walking at dusk
activities.description: Walk east along Shijo-dori into Gion. Hanamikoji-dori for teahouses. Possible maiko sighting

activities.name: Pontocho alley
activities.description: Atmospheric alley parallel to Kamo River

activities.name: P.M. Sounds
activities.description: Vinyl bar on Kiyamachi, near hotel

logistics.text: Kyoto is very walkable from your hotel — Gion, Pontocho, Nishiki Market, Kamo River all within 10-15 min
Kyoto buses are SLOW during Golden Week. Walk or use subway.
Don't forget: Buy matcha to take home at Ippodo Tea (Teramachi Street, 10 min from hotel). Scheduled for Day 7 but easy to pop in anytime — it's on the same street as Nishiki Market.
Kyoto Loft is in Mina Kyoto building on Kawaramachi — right by your hotel. 11am-9pm. Pop in anytime.

days.title: Fushimi Inari / Gion / Higashiyama
days.subtitle: Peak temple and culture day. Early start for empty torii gates (but not 6am — 7:30am).
days.summary: The iconic Kyoto day. Fushimi Inari's vermillion torii gates at 7:30am before the crowds, then a sake tasting flight in the historic brewing district. Walk the stone lanes to Kiyomizu-dera. Lunch is Bib Gourmand tempura. Afternoon is matcha everything — whisk your own at Ippodo (since 1717) and hit Assemblages for the famous parfait. Ramen for dinner, then Pontocho bars.

morningCoffee.name: Weekenders Coffee Tominokoji
morningCoffee.description: THE Kyoto coffee destination. Opens 7:30am. Closed Wed (today is Thu, perfect).

activities.name: Fushimi Inari
activities.description: By 7:30am for near-empty torii gates. The iconic vermillion tunnels. Free, open

activities.name: Brad:
activities.description: That's the money shot. No need to summit

activities.name: Fushimi Sake District
activities.description: Walk south from Inari into the historic sake brewing district. Get a flight of 18 local sakes at a tasting room

activities.name: Yasaka Shrine
activities.description: Free, always open

activities.name: Kiyomizu-dera
activities.description: UNESCO World Heritage. Famous wooden stage. Uphill but steady, not steep

activities.name: Kitenya
activities.description: Bib Gourmand tempura — Central location, walk-in friendly

activities.name: Yoshikawa Tempura
activities.description: Machiya with koi pond garden — Counter seats to watch the chef. Reserve ahead

sections.title: Afternoon — Tea + Rest
sections.subtitle: Alyona loves tea — this afternoon is for her:

activities.name: Ippodo Tea
activities.description: Since 1717. Whisk your own matcha at the counter with seasonal sweets. Teramachi Street, ~10 min walk from hotel. Also buy matcha to bring home

activities.name: Assemblages Kakimoto
activities.description: Award-winning matcha parfait. Open Thu-Mon. Closes 5pm cafe. Book ahead

activities.name: Menya Inoichi
activities.description: Bib Gourmand — Seafood shio ramen, 9 consecutive years. In Shimogyo-ku, ~10-15 min walk south from hotel. No train needed

activities.name: Wagyu Ramen Julia
activities.description: Gion — Walking distance from hotel

activities.name: L'Escamoteur
activities.description: Pontocho speakeasy with a magician-bartender. Near hotel

activities.name: Hello Dolly
activities.description: Jazz bar on Pontocho. Live music Fri/Sat but check Thu schedule

logistics.text: Fushimi Inari: trains start ~5:30 AM. The 7:30am arrival means leaving hotel ~7:00am.

🍳 Kichi Kichi Side Quest (try EVERY day, May 1-3): Chef Motokichi's legendary omurice in Pontocho. Tiny 14-seat counter. Same-day online reservations ONLY at kichikichi.com/kichikichi-reservation. Dinner slots open at 1:00 PM JST — they fill in 10-15 SECONDS. Both you and Alyona on the booking page at 12:59, refresh at 1:00, type fast. If you score it, dinner is 5-8:10PM (1-hour strict seating). ~5,000-8,000 yen/pp — get the regular omurice + beef tongue stew. Counter seats for the full tableside performance. Arrive 20 min early or lose your seat. Standby backup: show up at 4:30 PM (30 min before opening) and wait — no-shows happen because of their strict lateness policy. Try all 3 days. If you get it, swap that evening's dinner plan. The food is solid yoshoku, not life-changing, but the performance is genuinely one-of-a-kind.

days.title: Nara Day Trip
days.subtitle: Half-day in Nara (deer, giant Buddha), return for Kyoto afternoon and evening.
days.summary: Day trip to Nara — the one with the bowing deer and the world's largest bronze Buddha. Feed deer, gawk at Todai-ji, eat persimmon leaf sushi (a Nara-only thing). Back to Kyoto for a flat, easy walk along the Philosopher's Path and the gorgeous Nanzen-ji aqueduct. Dinner is unagi — eel over rice with a thin egg blanket. Then live jazz at Hello Dolly on Pontocho. It's Friday night, this is the one.

activities.name: Nara Park (Deer Park)
activities.description: Hundreds of free-roaming deer. Buy shika senbei (deer crackers, /pack). Deer bow if you bow to them!

activities.name: Todai-ji Temple
activities.description: World's largest bronze Buddha (15m). Jaw-dropping

activities.name: Kasuga Taisha Shrine
activities.description: Thousands of stone and bronze lanterns

activities.name: Naramachi
activities.description: Historic merchant district

activities.name: Kakinoha-zushi
activities.description: Nara specialty. A must-try

activities.name: Philosopher's Path
activities.description: Flat, easy 2km canal-side walk. Beautiful. Fine for Brad's foot

activities.name: Nanzen-ji
activities.description: Beautiful brick aqueduct (free photo op). Less crowded than Kiyomizu

activities.name: Kyogoku Kaneyo
activities.description: Unagi (eel) — Kinshi-don: eel over rice topped with thin egg omelet. Ritz-Carlton recommended. Closed Tue night/Wed. Open Fri

activities.name: Pontocho Fujita
activities.description: Kaiseki along Pontocho — River terraces may be open!

activities.name: Hello Dolly
activities.description: Live jazz Friday! Three sets at 8pm, 9:30pm, 11pm. Tonight is Friday — this is the night for it

activities.name: Jazz Live Candy
activities.description: Gion. Nightly live jazz. Can do both — Hello Dolly first, Candy after

logistics.text: Nara: walk from Kintetsu-Nara to Todai-ji is ~20-25 min on flat ground. Brad fine at relaxed pace.
Deer can be aggressive — keep crackers hidden until ready.

days.title: Constitution Memorial Day — Relaxed Day
days.subtitle: Golden Week peak. Many restaurants closed. Slow morning, explore what's open.
days.summary: The chill day. Sleep in, or go hunt for AMAM DACOTAN's insane artisan bread and the warabi mochi that takes 4 hours to knead. Mackerel pressed sushi near Yasaka Shrine for lunch. Afternoon is the haori shopping tour — five stores in walking distance, from affordable modern cuts to high-end indigo boro. Hamburger steak at a Kyoto cult favorite, then last night bars on Pontocho.

morningCoffee.name: % Arabica Higashiyama
morningCoffee.description: Go early (opens 8am). Stunning Yasaka Pagoda backdrop. *LINE UP: GW lines can be 20-30 min by 10am. Go right at opening.*

activities.name: AMAM DACOTAN Kyoto
activities.description: Artisan bakery in a gorgeous renovated machiya. ~120 varieties of creative bread from an Italian restaurant chef turned baker. 4-min walk from Karasuma Oike Station (10-15 min walk from hotel). Opens 8am. Register for the digital queue at 9:30am via Airwait (link on their IG @amam_dacotan_kyoto) — you'll get a number and can wander nearby until called. Must-grab: mentaiko peperoncino baguette, rare doughnut (banana brioche, limited daily), maritozzo. 2nd-floor cafe has % Arabica coffee in a beautiful machiya space (buy a drink to sit). Cashless OK. Check their IG the night before — they announce irregular closures. Golden Week crowds will be real, but the queue system means you're not standing in line

activities.name: Rakushaan (洛叉庵) Warabi Mochi
activities.description: Tabelog Top 100 Sweets WEST. Handmade warabi mochi, each batch kneaded for. Ethereal texture coated in kinako with black sugar syrup. Reservation-only, phone order required. Have the hotel concierge call 075-494-2551 the day before (May 2) to reserve up for the morning of May 3. up only, 11:30am-5pm. 8-piece box, 16-piece. Cash only. Location is in Shichiku (15 min from Kitaoji Station) — slightly out of the way, but near Daitoku-ji temple if you want to combine. Eat as soon as possible — the texture degrades within hours. Could up on the way to/from Arashiyama (Option A) or have Alyona grab it while you do something else. Best eaten in the hotel room on a plate — kinako goes everywhere

activities.name: Camellia Tea Ceremony
activities.description: Traditional matcha whisking in a beautiful machiya setting. Seasonal sweets included. Gion area

activities.name: Izuju
activities.description: Saba-zushi (mackerel pressed sushi) — Near Yasaka Shrine. Kyoto's signature sushi. Brad loves mackerel — this is a must

activities.name: Hiyoko Sushi
activities.description: 10-piece omakase from — Insane value. Locals' favorite

activities.name: Kodai-ji Temple
activities.description: Zen temple with stunning gardens

activities.name: Assemblages Kakimoto
activities.description: If you haven't done the matcha parfait yet

activities.name: Modern Haori Shopping Tour
activities.description: All stores walkable in central Nakagyo-ku (near Shinkyogoku/Gokomachi/Teramachi). Hit in one loop

activities.name: SOU SOU Kei-i (Menswear)
activities.description: Bold print haori jackets, Modern Japanese pop aesthetic. (If you didn't visit on Day 6)

activities.name: Musubi Kyoto (Shijo-Shinkyogoku)
activities.description: Clean, minimal modern haori designed to wear over t-shirts. 18 men's styles, The most affordable option. 12pm-8pm

activities.name: KAPITAL K.K-POPPO STORE
activities.description: Artisanal indigo/boro/sashiko noragi and haori. Fashion-forward Japanese-Americana. ~25,000-60,000+ yen. Walking distance from SOU SOU

activities.name: Visvim F.I.L. KYOTO
activities.description: Luxury tier. Handcrafted haori, noragi, kimono coats in premium fabrics. ~40,000-120,000+ yen. If budget isn't a concern, this is the pinnacle. 11am-7pm

activities.name: FDMTL at FASCINATE Kyoto
activities.description: Denim/indigo haori, sashiko-stitched. Grows character with wear

activities.name: Hikiniku to Come
activities.description: Charcoal-grilled hamburger patties with rice — Cult-favorite Kyoto spot. Walk-in, expect a queue. Near Kawaramachi

activities.name: Pontocho walk-in
activities.description: Browse the narrow alley, find a restaurant with kawayuka (river terrace) seating

activities.name: P.M. Sounds
activities.description: Vinyl bar, Kiyamachi. Near hotel

activities.name: L'Escamoteur
activities.description: Pontocho speakeasy, magician-bartender. If you haven't been yet

logistics.text: Constitution Memorial Day closures: Many family-run spots closed May 3-6.
Tomorrow you move to Osaka. Pack tonight.
Luggage forwarded via takkyubin (should have sent May 3 morning — arrange at front desk if not done).

days.title: Sunday, May 4 — Kyoto to Osaka — Greenery Day — Dotonbori / Shinsekai
days.subtitle: Travel to Osaka (~55 min). Afternoon exploring. Nakanoshima Festival starts today.
days.summary: Kyoto to Osaka on the Hankyu Line — under an hour, completely different energy. Osaka is louder, wilder, more neon. Lunch is okonomiyaki at a Dotonbori institution since 1945, then Shinsekai for retro alley vibes and kushikatsu (don't double-dip). Tower Knives for the knife nerds. Evening is the Nakanoshima Festival — a free handmade amusement park on an island — and Dotonbori lit up like Times Square on steroids.

morningCoffee.name: Weekenders Coffee Tominokoji
morningCoffee.description: One last Kyoto coffee. *(Closed Wed — Sun is fine)*

activities.name: Mizuno
activities.description: Dotonbori institution since 1945 — Mountain yam batter, incredibly fluffy. Walking distance from hotel. Expect 30-45 min GW wait
activities.lineUpTip: Arrive by 12:30pm. Expect 30-45 min GW wait.

activities.name: Ajinoya
activities.description: Bib Gourmand, Namba — More authentic, less tourist-heavy

activities.name: Shinsekai
activities.description: Retro 1912 neighborhood. Neon, dive bars

activities.name: Yaekatsu
activities.description: Kushikatsu est. 1949. Don't double-dip!

activities.name: Tower Knives Osaka
activities.description: Knife gallery. Free 10-min care lesson with purchase. Name engraving

activities.name: Kushikatsu Kei
activities.description: Renovated 80-year-old house — "Phantom sauce" with chicken broth. Beautiful setting

activities.name: Yaekatsu
activities.description: If you didn't eat there in the afternoon

activities.name: Nakanoshima Festival
activities.description: May 4-5, free! Handmade amusement park on the island. Check it out tonight!

activities.name: Dotonbori at night
activities.description: Neon-lit canal. Glico Running Man sign

logistics.text: Osaka Metro holiday day pass: 600 yen (weekend/holiday rate)
May 6 birthday dinner at Matsuzaka Gyu Yakiniku M Hozenji-Hanare at 7pm is CONFIRMED.
Namba Loft — 3F-4F Namba Center Building, near Namba Station. Open weekdays 11am-10pm, weekends 10am-10pm.
Shinsaibashi Loft — 4F of Daimaru Shinsaibashi. 10am-8:30pm.

days.title: Children's Day — Osaka Castle / Tenjinbashi / Shinsaibashi
days.subtitle: Golden Week continues. Explore Osaka's landmarks, food gems, and shopping.
days.summary: Osaka Castle in the morning (elevator, no stairs — Brad's foot is fine), then Japan's longest shopping arcade for the best cheap sushi you'll eat on this trip and Brad's Flower Mountain shoes. Afternoon is record shopping — Rare Groove is literally on the "World's Best Record Shops" list. Early ramen at a baseball-themed chicken-clam spot, then the main event: Casiopea live at Billboard Live Osaka. Japanese jazz fusion legends in an intimate venue. Speakeasy nightcap after.

morningCoffee.name: Takamura Wine & Coffee Roasters
morningCoffee.description: Wine shop + coffee roaster. Opens 11am. For pre-11am: Lilo Coffee (closer to hotel).

activities.name: Karukoma Sushi
activities.description: Tenjinbashi — Insane value. Toro, uni, anago. In Japan's longest shopping arcade. Expect 30-60 min GW wait. (Midosuji Line to Tenjinbashisuji-Rokuchome)
activities.lineUpTip: Arrive by 10:30-11:00am. Expect 30-60 min GW wait.

activities.name: Flower Mountain Tenjinbashi
activities.description: Same shopping arcade! 5-8-27 Tenjinbashi. Brad's shoe stop. Hit before or after sushi

activities.name: Osaka Castle
activities.description: Iconic landmark. Castle tower has elevator (no stairs for Brad)

activities.name: Grand Seiko Boutique Osaka Shinsaibashi
activities.description: If Houseki Hiroba (Apr 29) didn't have the piece. 4-amisenba, Osaka Toyota Building 1F. The Seiko; Grand Seiko combined floor here is the world's largest Seiko store (333+ m²). Tax-free with passport. ~45 min

activities.name: Nakanoshima Festival
activities.description: Day 2 if you didn't do it last night. Free! Also walk the beautiful Nakanoshima riverside — especially lovely lit up

activities.name: Shinsaibashi Shopping Arcade
activities.description: 180 stores. Onitsuka Tiger flagship, Don Quijote. Tax-free with passport. (Brad: Onitsuka Tiger here covers what we skipped in Tokyo.)

activities.name: Momotaro Jeans Osaka
activities.description: Horie neighborhood. If you haven't bought denim yet

activities.name: Japanese Jazz Vinyl — Shinsaibashi Record Run
Rare Groove
activities.description: On The Vinyl Factory's "World's Best Record Shops" list. Rare Japanese pressings specialty. ~8 min walk from hotel. Building has 5 other record shops too. Closed Wednesdays — today (Mon) is perfect

activities.name: HMV Record Shop Shinsaibashi
activities.description: 8F of Shinsaibashi OPA. 50,000 records, rated 5/5 for jazz. ~5 min from hotel

activities.name: Groovenut Records
activities.description: Amerikamura. Original pressings: soul, funk, jazz, rare groove. ~10 min from hotel

activities.name: Shoyu-Ramen Sodaisho
activities.description: Temma — Chicken-clam shoyu ramen. Baseball-themed. Local gem

activities.name: Chukasoba Kazura
activities.description: Awaza — "Mousse-like" broth, fabulously light

sections.title: Evening — Casiopea at Billboard Live Osaka

activities.name: Billboard Live Osaka
activities.description: Herbis Plaza ENT, Nishi-Umeda. Japanese jazz fusion legends. 7:00 PM show. Arrive by 6:30 for seating/drinks

activities.name: Bible Club Osaka
activities.description: Shinsaibashi speakeasy. Portland sister bar. Hidden entrance. Nightcap option

activities.name: Nakanoshima stroll
activities.description: Beautiful riverside walk from Umeda back toward hotel, lit up at night

logistics.text: Children's Day — similar closures to yesterday.
Tomorrow: More relaxed day before birthday dinner.

days.title: Brad's 40th Birthday!
days.subtitle: HAPPY BIRTHDAY BRAD! Sleep in, great coffee, birthday takoyaki, shopping, then the main event.
days.summary: Happy 40th. No alarm. Sleep in, birthday coffee, then Michelin-listed takoyaki — crispy outside, molten inside, best in the city. Afternoon is whatever you want — birthday shopping, kitchen street browsing, pure wandering. Pre-game cocktails with jazz and skyline views at the Waldorf Astoria 28th floor, then the main event: Matsuzaka wagyu yakiniku (confirmed 7pm). Finish at a speakeasy parfait bar.

activities.name: Takotsubo
activities.description: 5x Michelin-listed takoyaki — Crispy exterior, molten center. Away from tourist crowds

activities.name: Shinsaibashi Shopping
activities.description: Birthday shopping

activities.name: Doguyasuji Kitchen Street
activities.description: 5 min from hotel. Kitchen supplies, plastic food samples

activities.name: Canes & Tales at Waldorf Astoria
activities.description: 28th floor. Jazz, cocktails, Osaka skyline views. The perfect 40th birthday pre-game

activities.name: Matsuzaka Gyu Yakiniku M Hozenji-Hanare
activities.description: CONFIRMED 7:00 PM

activities.name: Ijou Maru Parfait (パフェと酒 以上。)
activities.description: THE birthday dessert. Members-only speakeasy parfait bar in Kitashinchi — but "membership" just means following @ijou_maru_parfait on Instagram (do this NOW). Elaborate art parfaits (each) paired with sake and cocktails, presented through a glowing reveal door. 15 seats. Holiday hours: 3pm-10:30pm. Cashless only. DM on Instagram to reserve — try in both English and Japanese. 3 min walk from Kitashinchi Station. The perfect surreal birthday cap after yakiniku. Follow the account now, DM a week before your visit

activities.name: Hozenji Yokocho
activities.description: Stone-paved, lantern-lit alley. Find a small bar for a birthday nightcap

activities.name: Bar Nayuta
activities.description: If the night is young

logistics.text: Birthday dinner is CONFIRMED. Arrive on time (7pm).
Ijou Maru Parfait: Follow @ijou_maru_parfait on Instagram BEFORE the trip. DM to reserve ~1 week ahead. Cashless only (Visa/MC/JCB/Amex/PayPay/IC cards all accepted).
Tomorrow: travel to Hakone. Pack tonight.

days.title: Thursday, May 7 — Osaka to Hakone — Travel Day / Ryokan
days.subtitle: Longest transit day. Shin-Osaka to Odawara by Shinkansen, then Hakone Tozan Railway to Gora.
days.summary: The big transition — Shinkansen from Osaka, then the scenic mountain switchback railway up to Gora. You arrive at the ryokan mid-afternoon and everything downshifts. Private onsen in your room, yukata robes, tatami floors. Tonight is multi-course kaiseki dinner served at the inn. After 12 days of going hard, this is where you exhale. The ryokan IS the destination.

activities.name: 9:15 AM
activities.description: Leave Cross Hotel. Walk to Namba Station, Midosuji Line to Shin-Osaka

activities.name: 10:18 AM
activities.description: Board Hikari 646 to Odawara (20 min)

activities.name: 12:38 PM
activities.description: Arrive Odawara Station

activities.name: Buy Hakone Free Pass
activities.description: 2-day at Odakyu counter

activities.name: Hakone Tozan Railway
activities.description: Odawara to Gora. Scenic switchback mountain railway

activities.name: Hakone Gora Byakudan
activities.description: Walking distance from Gora Station (or ask about up)

logistics.text: Ryokan IS the destination. Don't over-plan.

days.title: Open-Air Museum / Ropeway / Lake Ashi
days.subtitle: Full day in Hakone. Art, volcanic valley, lake cruise. All transport-based — minimal walking.
days.summary: The Hakone loop — all aboard. Open-Air Museum for Picassos and a hot spring foot bath, then cable car and ropeway up to the volcanic valley where you eat sulfur-boiled black eggs that supposedly add 7 years to your life. Mt. Fuji views if the sky cooperates. Pirate ship across Lake Ashi. All transport, minimal walking — the Free Pass covers everything. Second kaiseki dinner tonight, different menu from last night.

activities.name: Hakone Open-Air Museum
activities.description: Japan's first open-air sculpture museum. Major Picasso collection. Flat, paved paths. Hot spring foot bath on the grounds

activities.name: Gora to Sounzan
activities.description: Cable Car

activities.name: Sounzan to [Owakudani](https://maps.google.com/?q=Owakudani+Hakone)
activities.description: Ropeway. Volcanic steam vents. Mt. Fuji views on clear days

activities.name: Owakudani
activities.description: Eat the famous kuro-tamago (black eggs) (for 5). Short, flat walking

activities.name: Owakudani to Togendai
activities.description: Continue ropeway to the lake

activities.name: Lake Ashi Pirate Ship
activities.description: Cruise. Mt. Fuji reflection on clear days

activities.name: Hakone Roasting Coffee
activities.description: 3F for hand-dripped coffee with panoramic lake views

logistics.text: Hakone Free Pass covers ALL transport today
Check ropeway status in the morning (volcanic closures rare but possible)
Send luggage to Hotel MyStays Nippori via takkyubin this morning

days.title: Saturday, May 9 — Hakone to Tokyo — Nippori / Yanaka
days.subtitle: Golden Week is over. Quieter Tokyo. Hit missed spots. Last proper evening.
days.summary: Back to Tokyo for the final stretch. Golden Week is over, the city is calmer, and all those restaurants that were closed are open again. Early departure from Hakone for Pizza Bar on 38th (#2 pizza in the world) at 11:30 AM, then afternoon in Yanaka Ginza — cat-shaped everything, right by your hotel. Tonight is the last real dinner — Sushi Yoshikawa omakase with the otoro-uni dragon ball roll at 8:30 PM. Last night cocktails at Gen Yamamoto. Make it count.

morningCoffee.name: Ryokan breakfast, then coffee at Mandarin Oriental lobby before Pizza Bar.

activities.name: Arrive ~10:20 AM. Buffer time before 11:30 AM reservation.
Pizza Bar on 38th
activities.description: Omakase pizza (#2 in world) — 8 seats, Mandarin Oriental 38F

activities.name: Hotel MyStays Nippori
activities.description: 3-5 min walk from Nippori Station. Check in, drop bags, freshen up

activities.name: Yanaka Ginza
activities.description: Old-school shopping street right by hotel. Cat-shaped taiyaki, nostalgic charm

activities.name: Nakiryu ramen
activities.description: Post-GW, much shorter lines

sections.title: Dinner
sections.subtitle: Just Brad & Alyona now. Go wild.

activities.name: Sushi Yoshikawa
activities.description: Omakase — 17 pieces including "dragon ball roll" (otoro; uni) — (prepaid)

activities.name: Gen Yamamoto
activities.description: Cocktail omakase. Phone reservations only, 7 days ahead (call May 2 for May 9)

logistics.text: Golden Week is OVER. Restaurants that were closed are open again.
Both meals confirmed: Pizza Bar 11:30 AM, Sushi Yoshikawa 8:30 PM.
Pack tonight. Tomorrow is departure day.

days.title: Departure Day
days.subtitle: Last morning. Yanaka Ginza stroll, final food, then Skyliner to Narita. 5pm flight.
days.summary: Last morning in Japan. One final stroll through Yanaka Ginza for cat-tail donuts and taiyaki, maybe a quick lap through Ameyoko Market under the train tracks in Ueno. Skyliner from Nippori is a direct 36-minute shot to Narita — no stress. Buy Royce chocolate at duty-free (the good stuff needs refrigeration, airport only). Knives in checked bags. See you next time, Japan.

activities.name: Yanaka Ginza
activities.description: Morning stroll. Cat-tail donuts, fresh taiyaki

activities.name: Ameyoko Market
activities.description: 1 stop from Nippori. Chaotic market under the tracks. Last-minute snacks and gifts

logistics.text: Skyliner from Nippori is DIRECT to Narita — 36 min. Buy ticket at Keisei counter.
Royce Nama Chocolate needs refrigeration. Buy at airport.
Knives go in CHECKED luggage only.
Tax-free sealed goods: Don't open sealed consumable bags before leaving Japan.
